meta.title: The Gift That Demands a Return
meta.theme: why a gift in many societies obliges the receiver rather than freeing them

format.format_verified: 2026-07: five sentences, TITA, +3 with no negative marking, consistent in released papers 2016 to 2025; re-verify against each new official paper

traps.description: Trap B: the intruder shares gift, giver and obligation and opens with a demonstrative, but shifts from the anthropologist's analytic stance to a modern moralising complaint about consumer culture.

meta.learning_objective: notice a stance shift from analytic description to moral complaint under shared vocabulary

source.usage_note: Original composition in CAT register for CAT OS. General anthropology of gift exchange; no specific text adapted. Personal study only.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Odd One Out milestone); ODD_MAN_OUT_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: Advanced stance shift. The core describes gift exchange analytically; the outlier moralises about modern consumerism, sharing heavy vocabulary. Outlier at C. Owner review pending.

sentences.label: A
sentences.text: In many societies a gift is not the opposite of an exchange but a slower, subtler form of it, binding giver and receiver in a debt that time alone cannot cancel.

sentences.label: B
sentences.text: To accept is therefore to be placed under obligation, for the object arrives carrying something of the giver that the receiver must one day answer.

sentences.label: C
sentences.text: Today, of course, we have cheapened all this, drowning one another in thoughtless purchases that mean nothing and bind no one to anybody.

sentences.label: D
sentences.text: A return gift too prompt looks like repayment and insults the bond; one too delayed looks like forgetting and strains it, so the timing itself carries meaning.

sentences.label: E
sentences.text: The circle of giving, in short, is less a transfer of things than a way of keeping people tied to one another across the seasons of a life.

builder.prompt: Build the four sentence analysis of how the gift binds people together, from the debt to the circle. The sentence left out is your answer.

explanation.spine_note: The four sentences make one analytic argument about gift exchange. A states the thesis, a gift is a slow form of exchange that binds. B draws the obligation, accepting places you in debt. D shows the subtlety, even the timing of a return carries meaning. E concludes, the circle of giving keeps people tied across a life. Thesis, obligation, subtlety, conclusion, all in a describing, analysing voice.

core_roles.label: A
core_roles.why_here: It states the paragraph's thesis, the gift as a binding form of exchange, which every later sentence develops.

core_roles.label: B
core_roles.why_here: It draws the immediate consequence with therefore, that to accept a gift is to be placed under obligation.

core_roles.label: D
core_roles.why_here: It shows how fine the obligation is, the timing of a return carrying meaning, deepening B's claim with a concrete case.

core_roles.label: E
core_roles.why_here: It gathers the analysis into its point, the circle of giving as a way of keeping people tied across a life.

links.explanation: B opens with therefore, drawing the obligation directly from A's thesis that a gift binds giver and receiver.
B's obligation to answer is D's given, and D adds the new subtlety, that even the timing of the answer signifies.
E opens with in short, gathering the analysis of obligation and timing into its concluding image of the circle.

explanation.violation_named: Sentence C is a stance shift. The core analyses gift exchange in a describing, anthropological voice, neutral and explanatory throughout. C breaks into a moralising complaint about modern life, we have cheapened all this, thoughtless purchases that mean nothing. It judges where the paragraph explains, and it judges a different society, the modern consumer one, than the analysis describes.
explanation.why_it_breaks: Every core sentence advances the analysis of how gifts bind; the voice never evaluates, it accounts for. C evaluates, and it evaluates our own consumer culture, which the core never discusses. It is a lament, not a link. Nothing in the analysis sets up modern purchasing, and E's circle of giving takes up C's complaint not at all. An analysing paragraph has no seat for a sentence that stops analysing to disapprove.
explanation.trap_exposed: C is well disguised. It shares gift, cheapened and bind with the core, and its of course and today make it sound like a natural aside continuing the thought. Those are the hooks. But watch the direction of the voice: A, B, D and E describe and explain, while C complains and condemns. The words overlap; the stance reverses. A sentence that moralises inside an analysis has changed what the paragraph is doing, whatever vocabulary it carries.

exclusion_analysis.label: A
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: It is the longest and most abstract sentence, so before building, its role as the thesis rather than a summary is unclear.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without A the paragraph has no thesis: B's therefore draws a consequence from a claim that was never made.

exclusion_analysis.label: B
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: Its opening therefore can make it feel like a mere restatement of A, easy to think the paragraph could spare.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without B the key idea of obligation is gone, and D's talk of returns and bonds rests on a debt the paragraph never established.

exclusion_analysis.label: D
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: Its detailed rules about timing can read as an elaboration the argument does not strictly need.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without D the analysis loses its finest observation, the meaning carried by timing, and thins to a general claim.

exclusion_analysis.label: E
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: E and C both step back to comment on gift giving as a whole, so a hurried read may hold both and guess.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without E the analysis never lands its point, that the circle of giving keeps people tied across the seasons of a life.

explanation.detection_habit: Decide in one word what the paragraph is doing, describing or judging, and hold it against every sentence. A sentence that switches from analysis to complaint has left the paragraph, however much of its vocabulary it keeps.

mentor.challenge: Four sentences explain the gift. One disapproves of how we give today. Notice the moment the voice stops explaining.
mentor.takeaway: An analysis cannot use a sentence that moralises. Track what the paragraph does, not only what it discusses.
